# Scoped ignore list for the "Scan aws-cli (backup s3-upload container)"
# step in both ci.yml and scheduled-security-scan.yml ONLY (passed
# explicitly via trivy-action's `trivyignores` input on that one step) --
# NOT a repo-root .trivyignore, so it can't accidentally suppress a real
# finding in the app's own backend/frontend/postgres image scans.
#
# The s3-upload container (helm/team-manager/templates/backup-cronjob.yaml)
# runs exactly one command per invocation, in a loop over the pg_dump
# output file(s): `aws s3 cp /backup/<file> s3://<bucket>/<prefix>/<file>
# --region <region>` (optionally --endpoint-url). It never invokes an
# interactive shell, D-Bus, ACL tooling, or archive extraction -- all 19
# findings below are in OS packages incidentally present in the amazon/
# aws-cli base image, not exercised by that command.
#
# glib2 (7 CVEs) -- all in GLib entry points this container's `aws s3 cp`
# invocation never reaches: GVariant deserialization, GDateTime parsing,
# GRegex, GIOChannel line reading, GKeyFile parsing, and D-Bus SASL auth /
# introspection. This container never opens a D-Bus session or system bus
# (no SASL auth, no introspection XML), and never parses a GKeyFile-format
# config file -- aws-cli's own config/credentials here come from Kubernetes
# Secret env vars or IRSA, not a file GLib would parse.
CVE-2026-58010
CVE-2026-58011
CVE-2026-58012
CVE-2026-58013
CVE-2026-58014
CVE-2026-58015
CVE-2026-58016
#
# libacl (2 CVEs) -- require invoking getfacl/setfacl or the underlying
# libacl API to manipulate POSIX ACLs. This container's command never
# touches ACLs; it only performs an S3 file upload.
CVE-2026-54369
CVE-2026-54370
#
# python3 / python3-libs (5 CVEs, each reported once per package since aws-
# cli v2 bundles its own Python runtime):
# - CVE-2026-0864 (configparser injection via crafted multi-line input)
#   requires attacker-controlled content in a config file aws-cli parses;
#   credentials/config here come from Secret env vars or IRSA, never from
#   an untrusted file.
# - CVE-2026-11940 / CVE-2026-11972 (tarfile.extractall()/EOF-handling DoS)
#   require calling tarfile.extractall()/open() on an untrusted archive;
#   `aws s3 cp` never extracts a tar archive, it only uploads a file as-is.
# - CVE-2026-3276 (unicodedata DoS via excessive CPU consumption) requires
#   processing attacker-crafted Unicode text; the only "text" this
#   container handles is the pg_dump-generated filename
#   (teammanager-<timestamp>.dump, produced by the sibling pg-dump
#   initContainer) and Helm-templated, operator-controlled bucket/prefix/
#   region values -- none of it attacker-influenced.
# - CVE-2026-9669 (BZ2 decompression DoS) requires decompressing untrusted
#   bzip2 data; this container never decompresses anything.
CVE-2026-0864
CVE-2026-11940
CVE-2026-11972
CVE-2026-3276
CVE-2026-9669
#
# Re-check when Amazon publishes a rebuilt amazon/aws-cli:2.35.18 (or newer)
# digest carrying patched glib2/libacl/python3 packages, and re-pin
# helm/team-manager/values.yaml + both workflow files' image-ref then;
# remove the corresponding entries once that happens.
